message:
fix(router): Replace panic on invalid URI template with fail-safe fallback

Changes UriTemplate::new() to use graceful error handling instead of panicking
on invalid URI template patterns.

**Problem:**
The previous `Self::parse(pattern).expect("invalid URI template")` would crash
the entire server if a malformed URI template was registered, even if the
template was never actually used for routing.

**Solution:**
- Add `try_new()` method that returns Result for callers who want explicit
  error handling
- Modify `new()` to call `try_new()` and on failure:
  1. Log a warning with the invalid pattern and error details
  2. Return a non-matching fallback template (using "\0INVALID\0" literal
     that can never appear in real URIs)

This fail-safe approach ensures the server stays running even if configuration
includes a typo in a resource URI template. The warning log provides visibility
for debugging, while the non-matching template safely ignores requests that
would have matched the invalid pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
